examTitle: CEFR C1 Mock Reading & Use of English - Relationships & Family

parts.title: Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze on Family Dynamics
parts.instructions: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.
parts.content: The concept of family has undergone significant changes in recent years. (1) _______ (EVOLVE) as a result of increased mobility and changing social norms, families are no longer confined to traditional nuclear structures. (2) _______ (EMERGES) in various forms, such as single-parent households, blended families, and same-sex couples. This shift has led to a greater emphasis on flexibility and adaptability in family relationships. (3) _______ (REMAIN) a vital component of many societies, providing a sense of belonging and emotional support. However, the changing nature of family structures has also raised concerns about (4) _______ (STRENGTHEN) family bonds and the impact on children's well-being. (5) _______ (ACKNOWLEDGE) the importance of family in modern society, policymakers and researchers continue to explore innovative ways to support and empower families. (6) _______ (FACILITATE) communication and collaboration between family members, educational institutions, and community organizations can help address the challenges facing families today. (7) _______ (IMPROVE) family relationships requires a commitment to understanding and respecting the diversity of family structures and experiences. (8) _______ (SUPPORT) each other, families can build stronger, more resilient connections that foster a sense of community and social cohesion.

questions.question: Gap 1
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'A. Evolved' because the passage states that the concept of family has 'undergone significant changes' and 'evolved' as a result of increased mobility and changing social norms. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering multiple-choice cloze questions, carefully read the passage and identify the context in which the gap is located. Then, choose the option that best fits the meaning of the passage.

questions.question: Gap 2
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'B. Emerges' because the passage states that 'various forms of family' have 'emerged' as a result of changing social norms. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering multiple-choice cloze questions, pay attention to the verb tense and aspect used in the passage. In this case, the passage uses the present tense to describe the emergence of new family forms.

questions.question: Gap 3
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'A. Remain' because the passage states that 'family relationships' 'remain' a vital component of many societies. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering multiple-choice cloze questions, carefully read the passage and identify the context in which the gap is located. Then, choose the option that best fits the meaning of the passage.

questions.question: Gap 4
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'A. Weaken' because the passage states that the changing nature of family structures has raised concerns about 'weakening family bonds'. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering multiple-choice cloze questions, pay attention to the language used in the passage to describe the impact of changing family structures on family bonds.

questions.question: Gap 5
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'B. Acknowledge' because the passage states that 'policymakers and researchers continue to explore innovative ways to support and empower families, acknowledging the importance of family in modern society'. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering multiple-choice cloze questions, carefully read the passage and identify the context in which the gap is located. Then, choose the option that best fits the meaning of the passage.

questions.question: Gap 6
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'B. Facilitate' because the passage states that 'facilitating communication and collaboration between family members, educational institutions, and community organizations can help address the challenges facing families today'. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering multiple-choice cloze questions, pay attention to the language used in the passage to describe the actions that can help address the challenges facing families.

questions.question: Gap 7
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'A. Improve' because the passage states that 'improving family relationships requires a commitment to understanding and respecting the diversity of family structures and experiences'. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering multiple-choice cloze questions, carefully read the passage and identify the context in which the gap is located. Then, choose the option that best fits the meaning of the passage.

questions.question: Gap 8
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'A. Support' because the passage states that 'families can build stronger, more resilient connections that foster a sense of community and social cohesion when they support each other'. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering multiple-choice cloze questions, pay attention to the language used in the passage to describe the actions that families can take to build stronger connections.

parts.title: Part 2: Open Cloze on Family Relationships
parts.instructions: Read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap.
parts.content: The changing nature of family structures has led to a greater emphasis on flexibility and adaptability in family relationships. (1) _______ (EMERGE) in various forms, such as single-parent households, blended families, and same-sex couples. This shift has raised concerns about (2) _______ (STRENGTHEN) family bonds and the impact on children's well-being. However, the importance of family in modern society cannot be overstated, and (3) _______ (ACKNOWLEDGE) the diversity of family structures and experiences is crucial for building stronger connections.

questions.question: Gap 1
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'emerged' because the passage states that 'family structures have emerged in various forms'. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering open cloze questions, carefully read the passage and identify the context in which the gap is located. Then, choose the word that best fits the meaning of the passage.

questions.question: Gap 2
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'weaken' because the passage states that the changing nature of family structures has raised concerns about 'weakening family bonds'. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering open cloze questions, pay attention to the language used in the passage to describe the impact of changing family structures on family bonds.

questions.question: Gap 3
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'acknowledge' because the passage states that 'acknowledging the diversity of family structures and experiences is crucial for building stronger connections'. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering open cloze questions, carefully read the passage and identify the context in which the gap is located. Then, choose the word that best fits the meaning of the passage.

parts.title: Part 3: Word Formation on Family Relationships
parts.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.
parts.content: The changing nature of family structures has led to a greater emphasis on flexibility and adaptability in family relationships. The (1) _______ (DIVERSIFY) of family forms has raised concerns about the impact on children's well-being. However, the importance of family in modern society cannot be (2) _______ (UNDERMINED) by the changing nature of family structures. The key to building stronger connections between family members is to (3) _______ (ACKNOWLEDGE) the diversity of family structures and experiences.

questions.question: SIGNIFY
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'diversification' because the passage uses the word 'diversify' to describe the changing nature of family structures. The word 'diversification' is a noun form of 'diversify'. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering word formation questions, carefully read the passage and identify the context in which the gap is located. Then, choose the word that best fits the meaning of the passage.

questions.question: UNDERESTIMATE
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'undermined' because the passage uses the word 'undermine' to describe the impact of changing family structures on the importance of family in modern society. The word 'undermined' is a past participle form of 'undermine'. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering word formation questions, pay attention to the verb tense and aspect used in the passage. In this case, the passage uses the past participle 'undermined' to describe the impact of changing family structures.

questions.question: ACKNOWLEDGE
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'acknowledging' because the passage uses the word 'acknowledge' to describe the key to building stronger connections between family members. The word 'acknowledging' is a present participle form of 'acknowledge'. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering word formation questions, carefully read the passage and identify the context in which the gap is located. Then, choose the word that best fits the meaning of the passage.

parts.title: Part 4: Key Word Transformations on Family Relationships
parts.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given.

questions.question: The changing nature of family structures has led to a greater emphasis on flexibility and adaptability in family relationships. This shift has raised concerns about the impact on children's well-being. The key to building stronger connections between family members is to _______. (ACKNOWLEDGE)
questions.correctOption: acknowledge the diversity of family structures and experiences
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'acknowledge the diversity of family structures and experiences' because it accurately reflects the key to building stronger connections between family members as stated in the passage. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering key word transformation questions, carefully read the passage and identify the context in which the key word is located. Then, choose the phrase that best fits the meaning of the passage and uses the key word correctly.

questions.question: The importance of family in modern society cannot be overstated. However, the changing nature of family structures has raised concerns about the impact on children's well-being. _______ (UNDERESTIMATE) the importance of family in modern society is crucial for building stronger connections between family members.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'it is essential not to' because it accurately reflects the importance of family in modern society as stated in the passage. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering key word transformation questions, pay attention to the language used in the passage to describe the importance of family in modern society. In this case, the passage uses the phrase 'it is essential not to underestimate' to describe the importance of family in modern society.

parts.title: Part 5: Multiple-Choice Reading Comprehension on Family Relationships
parts.instructions: Read the text and answer the questions. For each question, choose the correct answer A, B, C or D.
parts.content: The changing nature of family structures has led to a greater emphasis on flexibility and adaptability in family relationships. This shift has raised concerns about the impact on children's well-being. However, the importance of family in modern society cannot be overstated. Family relationships are a vital component of many societies, providing a sense of belonging and emotional support. The changing nature of family structures has also raised concerns about the impact on children's well-being. Families are no longer confined to traditional nuclear structures, and the diversity of family forms has increased. This shift has led to a greater emphasis on flexibility and adaptability in family relationships.

questions.question: What does the writer suggest about the impact of changing family structures on children's well-being?
options: A. The impact is minimal and has no effect on children's well-being.
B. The impact is significant and has a detrimental effect on children's well-being.
C. The impact is neutral and has no effect on children's well-being.
D. The impact is positive and has a beneficial effect on children's well-being.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'B. The impact is significant and has a detrimental effect on children's well-being.' because the passage states that 'the changing nature of family structures has raised concerns about the impact on children's well-being'. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering multiple-choice reading comprehension questions, carefully read the passage and identify the context in which the question is located. Then, choose the option that best fits the meaning of the passage.

questions.question: What is the importance of family in modern society?
options: A. Family relationships are a vital component of many societies, providing a sense of belonging and emotional support.
B. Family relationships are not important in modern society.
C. Family relationships are a minor component of many societies.
D. Family relationships are not relevant in modern society.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'A. Family relationships are a vital component of many societies, providing a sense of belonging and emotional support.' because the passage states that 'family relationships are a vital component of many societies, providing a sense of belonging and emotional support'. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering multiple-choice reading comprehension questions, pay attention to the language used in the passage to describe the importance of family in modern society. In this case, the passage uses the phrase 'family relationships are a vital component of many societies' to describe the importance of family in modern society.

options: A. The impact is minimal and has no effect on children's well-being.
B. The impact is significant and has a detrimental effect on children's well-being.
C. The impact is neutral and has no effect on children's well-being.

parts.title: Part 6: Cross-Text Multiple Matching on Family Relationships
parts.instructions: Read the four texts about the topic. For Questions 1-6, choose from the writers A-D.
parts.content:
Writer A:
The changing nature of family structures has led to a greater emphasis on flexibility and adaptability in family relationships. This shift has raised concerns about the impact on children's well-being.

Writer B:
Family relationships are a vital component of many societies, providing a sense of belonging and emotional support. However, the changing nature of family structures has also raised concerns about the impact on children's well-being.

Writer C:
The importance of family in modern society cannot be overstated. Family relationships are a vital component of many societies, providing a sense of belonging and emotional support.

Writer D:
The changing nature of family structures has led to a greater emphasis on flexibility and adaptability in family relationships. However, the importance of family in modern society cannot be overlooked.

questions.question: Which writer agrees with the idea that family relationships are a vital component of many societies?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'C' because Writer C states that 'the importance of family in modern society cannot be overstated' and that 'family relationships are a vital component of many societies'. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering cross-text multiple matching questions, carefully read the passage and identify the context in which the question is located. Then, choose the writer who agrees with the idea being discussed.

questions.question: Which writer disagrees with the idea that family relationships are a vital component of many societies?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'B' because Writer B states that 'the changing nature of family structures has also raised concerns about the impact on children's well-being' without emphasizing the importance of family relationships. The other options do not accurately reflect the meaning of the passage.
questions.tips: When answering cross-text multiple matching questions, pay attention to the language used in the passage to describe the importance of family relationships. In this case, Writer B does not emphasize the importance of family relationships.

parts.title: Part 7: Gapped Text on Family Relationships
parts.instructions: Six paragraphs have been removed from the text. Choose from the paragraphs A-G the one which fits each gap. There is one extra paragraph which you do not need to use.
parts.content: The changing nature of family structures has led to a greater emphasis on flexibility and adaptability in family relationships. [Gap 1] This shift has raised concerns about the impact on children's well-being. However, the importance of family in modern society cannot be overstated. Family relationships are a vital component of many societies, providing a sense of belonging and emotional support. [Gap 2] The changing nature of family structures has also raised concerns about the impact on children's well-being. [Gap 3] The key to building stronger connections between family members is to acknowledge the diversity of family structures and experiences. [Gap 4] Families are no longer confined to traditional nuclear structures, and the diversity of family forms has increased. [Gap 5] This shift has led to a greater emphasis on flexibility and adaptability in family relationships.
